snippet.title: China and the World: Schneider Electric CEO on sustainable business
snippet.description:
For  more:
https://www.cgtn.com/video

How to make a company more sustainable is a matter of survival for entrepreneurs today. So CGTN anchor Tian Wei asked Jean-Pascal Tricoire, the chairman and CEO of Schneider Electric, how his company is doing. He also shared his views about digitalization, which is both an opportunity and a new challenge for today's business, as well as some interesting stories about his father.

snippet.title: 外企看好中国市场 携手推进数字化发展
snippet.description: 生机勃勃的中国市场，正向世界释放强大磁力。在中国发展高层论坛2023年年会上，施耐德电气董事长兼首席执行官赵国华接受访谈专访，围绕如何看待中国未来的经济前景、中国经济对外资企业的吸引力等问题，讲述施耐德电气在华投资的经验和感受。

snippet.title: Data Center Insights: Episode 18, featuring Red Engineering’s CEO, Martin Sieh | Schneider Electric
snippet.description:
RED Engineering is an MEP service provider that helps clients realize its plans for zero carbon built environments. In this interview from Schneider Electric’s Data Center Insights series, CEO for RED Engineering, Martin Sieh, explains how the data center industry’s move towards zero-carbon is redefining the landscape in terms of design and construction in the marketplace. Sieh describes how the industry is being driven by changing customer demands for modular data centers that are designed with sustainability in mind, and he highlights the benefits of partnering with Schneider Electric in  driving energy-efficient data center innovations. Finally, Sieh gives his thoughts on how he sees the data center industry evolving over the next five years.

snippet.title: Smart meters and data enablers of the energy transition - Landis+Gyr CEO Werner Lieberherr
snippet.description:
Smart meters are necessary as the grid is becoming much more dynamic and both utilities and consumers need the better information they provide, said Werner Lieberherr, CEO of Landis+Gyr.

Speaking in a Connect interview at Enlit Europe, Lieberherr highlighted the importance of accelerating the smart meter rollout in Europe and changing legislation to enable it.

snippet.title: Digital Transformation with AI
snippet.description:
Overview
In this episode of the Data Bytes podcast, we will be joined by Gaston Besanson, the Global Head of Data Science at PepsiCo. Gaston has been building new practices, businesses, and high-performing teams in data science for more than 18 years and driving business evolution through data processing, data analytics, and decision support. In this interview, we will explore the topic of digital transformation and its key barriers. We will also discuss the role of the global data science team in PepsiCo, what defines an ideal PepsiCo data scientist, and the types of problems that Gaston's teams work on today. Additionally, we will touch upon the importance of diversity at the time of building analytics solutions. Tune in to learn more about how AI is driving digital transformation at PepsiCo.


About Gaston Besanson


Gaston Besanson has been building new practices, businesses and high-performing teams in data science for more than 18 years and driving business evolution through data processing, data analytics and decision support. After more than a decade leading teams in various private and academic institutions, since early 2021 Gaston joins PepsiCo as Global Head of Data Science to build a global big data science team working across the company's different functions of interest. Since November 2021, he leads PepsiCo's Global Digital Hub in Barcelona.


The focus of Gaston's work is the continuous development of AI-based analytics components as part of PepsiCo's digital products and services, along with innovation in the field of intelligent systems applied to business processes. This is coupled with his strong values of fostering a respectful and dynamic culture based on empowerment, communication and collaboration.
